docs.title: Amazon Associate Tag Setup
docs.snippet:
# Amazon Associate Tag Setup

Use this procedure when a creator's Amazon links aren't carrying their associate tag, or they ask how commissions work.

## How it works
When a captured product
docs.body:
# Amazon Associate Tag Setup

Use this procedure when a creator's Amazon links aren't carrying their associate tag, or they ask how commissions work.

## How it works
When a captured product URL is a real `amazon.com` link, Shopfolio automatically appends the creator's **associate tag** so qualifying purchases are attributed to them. Non-Amazon merchants carry **no** commission — Shopfolio does not mint or inject affiliate links for other retailers.

## Setup
1. In **Settings → Integrations → Amazon**, have the creator paste their Amazon Associates **tracking ID** (the `tag=` value).
2. Save. From then on, new Amazon captures get the tag applied at redirect time on the `/r/<code>` link.

## Troubleshooting
1. **Tag missing on a link:** confirm the tracking ID is saved and the destination is a genuine `amazon.com` URL (shortened `amzn.to` links should be expanded first).
2. **Links captured before the tag was added:** re-save the item to re-apply, or bulk **Re-apply tag** from the library.

## Notes
- Shopfolio takes no cut; the associate relationship is directly between the creator and Amazon.

docs.title: Link Click Analytics
docs.snippet:
# Link Click Analytics

Use this procedure when a creator asks about clicks, why a link shows no data, or what the analytics do and don't track.

## What we track
Every storefront and post l
docs.body:
# Link Click Analytics

Use this procedure when a creator asks about clicks, why a link shows no data, or what the analytics do and don't track.

## What we track
Every storefront and post link is served through a tracked `/r/<code>` redirect. We count **click-throughs** — opens per link, over time, by referrer. That's it.

## What we do NOT track
Shopfolio does **not** track sales, revenue, or dollar amounts. Click analytics are revenue-free by design. If a creator expects to see earnings, explain that monetization is intentionally out of scope and only click data is available.

## Troubleshooting "no clicks"
1. Confirm the link in the creator's bio/post is the `/r/<code>` short link, not the raw destination URL — raw links bypass tracking.
2. Analytics roll up on a short delay; brand-new links can read zero for a few minutes.
3. Ad blockers and some in-app browsers suppress the redirect ping, so counts are a floor, not an exact number.

## Escalate when
- A link that clearly gets traffic reports zero for more than an hour.

docs.title: Support Escalation and Outages
docs.snippet:
# Support Escalation and Outages

Use this procedure to decide when to escalate a Shopfolio support case beyond first-line help.

## Escalate immediately
- **Storefronts down for many creato
docs.body:
# Support Escalation and Outages

Use this procedure to decide when to escalate a Shopfolio support case beyond first-line help.

## Escalate immediately
- **Storefronts down for many creators at once** — a custom-domain edge or certificate incident affecting more than one account.
- **The publish queue is backed up across creators** — jobs stuck `processing` or `pending` platform-wide (see *Scheduled Posts Not Publishing*).
- **A real billing charge naming Shopfolio** — even though billing isn't live (see *Billing and Subscription*).
- **Any suspected data exposure or account takeover.**

## Handle first, escalate only if unresolved
- Single custom-domain SSL/DNS setup (see *Storefront SSL / TLS Certificate Issues*) — escalate only if a corrected, unproxied record still won't provision after 2+ hours.
- Individual capture, import, or analytics quirks.

## What to include when you escalate
1. Creator's account email and the affected domain or post/link.
2. Exact state shown in the dashboard (`DNS pending`, `verifying`, `pending`, `failed`, etc.) and timestamps.
3. What you already tried and the result.

## Tone
Lead with the impact on the creator's audience or drop, not the internal cause. Give a concrete next-check time rather than an open-ended "we're looking into it."

docs.title: Fixing Broken or 404 Links
docs.snippet:
# Fixing Broken or 404 Links

Use this procedure when a creator's product link returns a 404, redirects to a homepage, or shows as `broken` in their Library.

## How link checking works
- Sh
docs.body:
# Fixing Broken or 404 Links

Use this procedure when a creator's product link returns a 404, redirects to a homepage, or shows as `broken` in their Library.

## How link checking works
- Shopfolio periodically re-checks destination links and flags each as `ok`, `redirected`, or `broken` on the Library card.
- A `broken` flag means the merchant returned a 404 or the URL no longer resolves.

## Steps to fix
1. Open **Library** and filter by **Link status → Broken**.
2. For each item, open the destination to confirm it's dead, then paste a working replacement URL and save.
3. If the product moved, capture the new page with the Chrome extension to refresh image and price too.

## Common issues
- **`redirected` but works:** the merchant added tracking or region redirects — usually safe to leave.
- **Whole retailer flagged:** the merchant changed its URL structure; use bulk retargeting (see *Bulk Editing and Retargeting Links*).

## Escalate when
- Links report `broken` in Shopfolio but load fine in a browser (possible checker false-positive).

docs.snippet:
# Amazon Associate Tag Setup

Use this procedure when a creator's Amazon links aren't carrying their associate tag, or they ask how commissions work.

## How it works
When a captured product
docs.body:
# Amazon Associate Tag Setup

Use this procedure when a creator's Amazon links aren't carrying their associate tag, or they ask how commissions work.

## How it works
When a captured product URL is a real `amazon.com` link, Shopfolio automatically appends the creator's **associate tag** so qualifying purchases are attributed to them. Non-Amazon merchants carry **no** commission — Shopfolio does not mint or inject affiliate links for other retailers.

## Setup
1. In **Settings → Integrations → Amazon**, have the creator paste their Amazon Associates **tracking ID** (the `tag=` value).
2. Save. From then on, new Amazon captures get the tag applied at redirect time on the `/r/<code>` link.

## Troubleshooting
1. **Tag missing on a link:** confirm the tracking ID is saved and the destination is a genuine `amazon.com` URL (shortened `amzn.to` links should be expanded first).
2. **Links captured before the tag was added:** re-save the item to re-apply, or bulk **Re-apply tag** from the library.

## Notes

docs.title: Capturing Products with the Extension
docs.snippet:
# Capturing Products with the Extension

Use this procedure when a creator can't save a product while browsing, or asks how capture works.

## How capture works
Every capture path — the Chro
docs.body:
# Capturing Products with the Extension

Use this procedure when a creator can't save a product while browsing, or asks how capture works.

## How capture works
Every capture path — the Chrome extension, the PWA share sheet, manual add, CSV import, and user-initiated page snapshots — funnels through a single `captureLink()` entry point. It fetches the page, pulls the title, image, price, and canonical URL, and drops the item into the creator's library as a draft.

## Troubleshooting
1. **"Couldn't read this page":** some merchants block server-side fetches. Have the creator use the **extension** (which reads the page they're already on) or the **snapshot** option rather than pasting a bare URL.
2. **Wrong image or price:** open the item and use **Edit → Re-fetch**, or set the fields manually. Manual edits are never overwritten by a later re-fetch.
3. **Extension not capturing:** confirm it's signed into the same workspace and has permission for the current site; pin it and retry.

## Notes
- Captured items are private drafts until added to a post or storefront.
- Amazon URLs get the creator's associate tag automatically (see *Amazon Associate Tag Setup*).

docs.body:
# Fixing Broken or 404 Links

Use this procedure when a creator's product link returns a 404, redirects to a homepage, or shows as `broken` in their Library.

## How link checking works
- Shopfolio periodically re-checks destination links and flags each as `ok`, `redirected`, or `broken` on the Library card.
- A `broken` flag means the merchant returned a 404 or the URL no longer resolves.

## Steps to fix
1. Open **Library** and filter by **Link status → Broken**.
2. For each item, open the destination to confirm it's dead, then paste a working replacement URL and save.
3. If the product moved, capture the new page with the Chrome extension to refresh image and price too.

## Common issues
- **`redirected` but works:** the merchant added tracking or region redirects — usually safe to leave.
- **Whole retailer flagged:** the merchant changed its URL structure; use bulk retargeting (see *Bulk Editing and Retargeting Links*).

## Escalate when

docs.title: Storefront SEO and Metadata
docs.snippet:
# Storefront SEO and Metadata

Use this procedure when a creator wants their storefront to rank or preview better — page titles, descriptions, or social share cards look wrong.

## What we c
docs.body:
# Storefront SEO and Metadata

Use this procedure when a creator wants their storefront to rank or preview better — page titles, descriptions, or social share cards look wrong.

## What we control
- Each storefront has an SEO title, meta description, and an Open Graph image used for link previews.
- We auto-generate a sitemap and serve it to search engines behind Cloudflare.

## Steps
1. In **Storefront → SEO**, have the creator set the page title (under ~60 characters) and meta description (under ~155).
2. Upload or confirm the Open Graph image — this is what shows when the link is shared on social.
3. Save; changes are live immediately, but search engines re-crawl on their own schedule.

## Common issues
- **Share preview shows old image:** the social platform cached it — use that platform's URL debugger to refresh.
- **Not appearing in search:** new storefronts take days to index; confirm the storefront isn't set to private.

## Escalate when
- The sitemap returns an error or search engines report crawl failures on the domain.

docs.title: Importing from LTK and ShopMy
docs.snippet:
# Importing from LTK and ShopMy

Use this procedure when a creator wants to bring an existing catalog over from LTK, ShopMy, or a similar tool.

## What's supported
These are **import-only**
docs.body:
# Importing from LTK and ShopMy

Use this procedure when a creator wants to bring an existing catalog over from LTK, ShopMy, or a similar tool.

## What's supported
These are **import-only** sources. Shopfolio can pull a creator's existing products and links in as captured items so they can rebuild their storefront here. There is **no live, two-way integration** — we don't sync back, and we don't post to those platforms.

## How to import
1. Export the catalog from the source tool (CSV or the platform's export), or paste the profile/collection URL.
2. In **Library → Import**, choose the source and upload the file or URL. Items come in as drafts through the same `captureLink()` path.
3. Review and fix any items that failed to fetch, then add them to a storefront or post.

## Notes
- Amazon links in the import get the creator's associate tag applied (see *Amazon Associate Tag Setup*); other merchants carry no commission.
- Because it's import-only, disconnecting the source later doesn't remove already-imported items.

## Escalate when
- A large import (1000+ items) stalls partway.

docs.title: Bulk Editing and Retargeting Links
docs.snippet:
# Bulk Editing and Retargeting Links

Use this procedure when a creator needs to change many links at once — for example swapping an affiliate tag or retargeting a whole retailer that change
docs.body:
# Bulk Editing and Retargeting Links

Use this procedure when a creator needs to change many links at once — for example swapping an affiliate tag or retargeting a whole retailer that changed URLs.

## What bulk editing can do
- Select multiple Library items and apply one change: append or replace an affiliate tag, swap a domain, or move them to a collection.
- Changes preview before they apply so the creator can confirm the count.

## Steps
1. In **Library**, use the checkboxes (or **Select all** with a filter) to choose the items.
2. Open **Bulk actions → Retarget links** and choose the operation (e.g. replace `oldshop.com` with `newshop.com`).
3. Review the preview count and confirm — the change runs in the background and updates each card's link status.

## Common issues
- **Some items skipped:** their URLs didn't match the find pattern; narrow the filter and retry.
- **Wrong tag applied:** re-run with the correct tag — bulk edits are not versioned, so double-check before confirming.

## Escalate when
- A bulk operation reports success but the Library still shows old links after a refresh.
